content:
{
  "title": "Execution-Aware Prompting: Leveraging Partial Program Traces for Enhanced Code Generation with LLMs",
  "problem": "Current code generation prompting methods focus on natural language reasoning or program planning but largely neglect explicit integration of executable feedback within the prompt. There is limited exploration of prompting LLMs with partial program traces to guide stepwise, execution-aware code synthesis.",
  "motivation": "Incorporating execution feedback into code generation can help LLMs avoid common logical and syntax errors, producing more robust and semantically correct code. Existing prompting methods do not leverage the powerful signal provided by partial program outputs or traces, missing an opportunity to steer generation toward correctness as observed during program execution.",
  "method": "We propose Execution-Aware Prompting (EAP), a new method where prompts supply LLMs with partial code fragments and their actual or simulated execution traces (e.g., outputs or intermediate variable values), requesting the model to continue generation that is consistent with observed behavior. EAP differs from prior CoT or planning approaches by explicitly grounding code synthesis in concrete, execution-derived feedback rather than pure reasoning or self-revision. During training and inference, we sample or synthesize partial programs, run them to obtain traces, and embed these trace-program pairs in the prompt, asking the model to extend the program to complete the desired function while preserving correctness relative to the given traces.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate EAP on HumanEval and MBPP, as well as selected real-world code repositories. Baselines include standard code generation, chain-of-thought, and planning-based prompting. Metrics are code correctness (pass@k), syntax validity, and semantic alignment with traces. Ablations will test the contribution of trace detail granularity and trace-program pairing strategies."
}
